title: Fold-safe specialist stack with rank blending, not raw-prob averaging
summary: Stacked generalization helps when base learners have different biases.
rationale: Stacked generalization helps when base learners have different biases. Here, one model can specialize in normal slick stints, another in wet compounds or endgame laps, and another in rare-driver/high-cardinality categorical structure; AUC usually benefits more from OOF rank blending than from over-tuned probability calibration.
implementation_hint: Train 2-3 diverse OOF models with disjoint inductive biases: current heuristic-rich GBDT baseline, native-categorical CatBoost, and the hazard/ranking variant above. Blend OOF predictions by regime (`slick`, `wet`, `late race`, `stint<=3 laps after last stop`) using rank averages or a very small logistic meta-model trained only on OOF folds. Validate with purged/grouped race CV.
expected_effect: Should improve robustness and public LB transfer by combining complementary ranking behavior instead of chasing one global model.
risk: If CV is not purged by race and temporal neighborhood, the stacker will overstate gains; regime gating can overfit small subsets.
